raw_input:
[3.0/5] Not the best quality, would be great for a younger child or on the go A little disappointed in this package. I'll start with the good, the gloves work nicely and fit comfortably. The gloves are soft and easy to adjust on the hand and they're lightweight too so they don't get hot and make your hands sweaty.

The standing lamp is a micro USB cable and receiver for power. There is flex when I gently push the top of the lamp and the base. It feels really fragile. The light is a nice soft blue and doesn't get hot when it's on, the power button is fairly easy to press.

The individual handheld lamp isn't chargeable. It runs off three AAA batteries. The hand lamp is a deep blue light but it seems feint. The pink grips also slide on and off the casing fairly easily. It feels really lightweight and cheaper rather than a quality lightweight material.

I think this would be a great kit for travel, you wouldn't be devastated if you lost or damaged and had to replace these tools or maybe for a younger child who is starting to express interest in gel manicures. You wouldn't mind if there's some wear on these and you would replace them with nice quality items when they get used to handling them. This is the nail kit equivalent of giving your cousin your MadCatz controller instead of the official Nintendo one. It's good to have but I think it's worth it to throw a little more into the savings bank and buying a higher quality set or individual set of lamps. [5.0/5] Nail lamp Very nice nail lamp and flash light. I like the pink color of the flash light lol but they work well and are portable. Perfect for flash curing too. [5.0/5] Nail Lamp set Very pleased with this versatile set. These are not as powerful as a full hand, high wattage nail lamp is, but it has its uses.

2 UV LED nail lamps, charging cable, and a pair of UV protective gloves. 3 AAA batteries are not included to power the flashlight lamp.

The white lamp comes with a USB charging cord & must be plugged in for use. Quick on and off button easily accessed with the fingertip being cured. While this is for one finger at a time, if you have small fingertips, two to three nails fit comfortably. Keep in mind, this is lower wattage than the full hand lamps, so curing time may be longer. This is great for the end stages of nail art, quickly curing rhinestones or painted accents.

The pink flashlight style nail lamp is not only great for nail art but also for crafts. Portable, battery-operated. Works well with nail supplies but also with epoxy, curing craft glues, and other craft needs. It's especially fabulous when fixing a nail on-the-go. The silicone sleeve allows for accuracy when holding and operating the flashlight.

The gloves are great at protecting your hands when doing a manicure, especially when using the full hand nail lamps where your entire hand is exposed to the UV & LED lights at a high wattage. They're not the best quality, but they're exactly the same quality of all the pairs I already have. A few stray strings that are easily clipped, a thin satin material that protects the skin to being exposed to direct UV light. The finger portions can be rolled down for those with shorter fingers. [3.0/5] Cheap, but it works. Gloves are awful. I ordered the three-piece set with lamp, flashlight, and gloves. The gloves are super, god-awful cheap. The material is so thin it is see-through... and if you can see through something -- that means light is passing through it. I wouldn't trust the gloves to protect me at all and you shouldn't either. Thankfully, I already own much better nail lamp gloves and I can just throw these away. The LED lamp and flashlight both work, with the LED lamp powered by USB (it must be plugged in) and the LED flashlight powered by three AAA batteries. The flashlight is super cheap and I don't expect that to last either; it's made of Aluminum -- I think -- and it has these silicone covers on them that slide around and I don't like that as it's annoying when they slide around.

Despite all my complaints -- the lights work. Range of light is 365 to 405 nm, which is near-UV to visible (violet), with near-UV meaning UV wavelengths are near the visible border closer to the violet side. These UV LED lights are cheap, common, and they work, so no complaints there. I'll update this review if this product breaks down, but for now, I give it three stars for "It's OK. It works but the quality is seriously lacking." [3.0/5] Cheap but gets it done OK so this is def a cheap uv light set. The flashlight is a blue light flashlight that is actually pretty bright and you can use it to cure your nails on the go I guess. The mini uv lamp is very tiny. One finger at a time tiny. I like that I don't have to put my whole hand under a lamp but it does make the process take longer. The gloves are pretty flimsy. You can see thru them so if they are meant to protect your skin from the uv then it's not gonna work. [2.0/5] Just received a light…it’s okay. Only received the light so I can’t really comment on the other things meant to be in the packet. But the light seems to work fine so far… it’s not automatic but the on and off finger pressure is easy enough to control [3.0/5] Has to be plugged in The photos on the description make it seem like this nail lamp does not have to be plugged in making it truly portable to use anywhere. That is not the case, it has dmto be plugged in to work not to charge as it says in the description. If that is not a deal breaker for you, then I will say that this is a decent lamp if you want to do each nail 1 at a time. In my daughter's case it works good to make nail charms although there are often some that come out squishy and not completely dried. I have not tried the flashlight yet because I keep forgetting to buy the required batteries [5.0/5] Amazing! The size of the LED light is so convienent! I also love the little blue light! Both I use when doing my nails and designing them! [3.0/5] Great if you are just starting out doing your own gel nails. A few things to note with this little device: batteries are not included in the flashlight so you will need 3 AAA batteries ready. The desk unit must be plugged into use, the cord is about 30” long, and the desk unit stays on for 1 minute before it shuts off (you can turn it back on but it does shut off at 1 minute). The unit is small and will fit one finger at a time (maybe two if you have thin fingers) and the gloves are one size fits all which means they were too big for me, in the picture I had to scrunch down each finger and they kept sliding into my nail.

The weren’t really any instructions included, but using this product isn’t rocket science - plug it into a usb brick or outlet, plug it into the outlet and push the power button.

This would be a cheap and easy way to see if doing your own nails is something you want to do, but for the more experienced, one nail at a time might get frustrating. [4.0/5] Mini set This mini set is good. Easy to travel with and work on the go. Tiny but does big work. [5.0/5] Convenient Push Button - Gloves Nice and Long Syle: White Lamp, Black Gloves, UV Flashlight
This mini Lamp works well and I like the position of. The push/power button being right on top of the base where your finger sits. This way I can hold my tip in place with my free hand while pushing the power button with my finger being cured. Not quite the same as hands-free touch sensor, but nearly close to it. I really loved the Gloves and surprised they not only fit my hand (long fingers) but that the ends reach all the way to the base of my nails, affording maximum skin protection. I really hasn't expected them to fit so well. If you have much shorter fingers you may have to 'cuff' them to fit shorter. The Flashlight works fine and I can use it for quick flash cure to set my Gel on each Nail and then full-cure under Lamp. Overall a nice combo set. [5.0/5] Great Mini UV Lights! These 2PCS Mini UV LED Lights are Excellent!
Both are easy and very convenient to use! I especially love the hand held flashlight design of this mini UV light. It's small enough to throw in my purse or even sit on the couch and do nail art to my nails. This set of mini lights comes with a pair of UV protection gloves.
I love this set!
Great Value!

response:
Here is a summary of the product reviews:

Overall, customers have mixed feelings about this nail lamp set, which includes a standing USB lamp, a handheld flashlight-style lamp, and UV protective gloves. The set averages around 3.5 to 4 stars across reviews.

On the positive side, many customers appreciate the portability and convenience of the set, particularly for travel or on-the-go use. The standing lamp's power button placement is praised for being easy to use while curing nails. Some customers find the set great for nail art details, curing rhinestones, and even craft projects like epoxy. Several reviewers note it is a good starter kit for beginners exploring gel manicures.

However, there are consistent complaints across multiple reviews. The gloves are widely criticized for being too thin and see-through, raising concerns about whether they actually protect skin from UV light. The standing lamp must be plugged in to work rather than being truly portable, which disappointed some buyers. The handheld flashlight requires 3 AAA batteries that are not included. Both lights are described as feeling cheap and fragile, and the silicone covers on the flashlight tend to slide around annoyingly. The standing lamp only fits one finger at a time, making the curing process slower.

Most reviewers agree that while the lights do function as intended, the overall quality is low. The set is best suited for beginners, younger users, or occasional travel use rather than serious or experienced nail enthusiasts.
